sentence: Sitting in my morning class, I wondered why I even bothered showing up.
label: disengaged

sentence: I got my midterm grade back and felt absolutely nothing, not even disappointment.
label: disengaged

sentence: My therapist asked what I was looking forward to, and I genuinely couldn't think of anything.
label: disengaged

sentence: I used to love writing, but now I stare at blank documents and feel this weird emptiness.
label: disengaged

sentence: My laundry has been piling up for two weeks and I keep walking past it like it's not there.
label: disengaged

sentence: I ate lunch today without tasting any of it, just going through the motions.
label: disengaged

sentence: Someone complimented my project and I thought, "Cool, I guess," with absolutely zero pride.
label: disengaged

sentence: I keep forgetting why I chose this major in the first place.
label: disengaged

sentence: My parents called to check in and I gave them the shortest answers possible.
label: disengaged

sentence: I showed up to my part-time job but spent the shift counting down the minutes until I could leave.
label: disengaged

sentence: Even when my medication is working, I feel like I'm just existing, not actually living.
label: disengaged

sentence: I have three essays due next week, two exams I haven't studied for, and my therapist wants me to start journaling, but I can't even remember where I put my planner.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: My roommate is talking to me about their weekend while my phone is buzzing with Discord notifications, my professor is emailing about the assignment I forgot, and I'm trying to eat cereal.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I sat down to write one paragraph and now it's 2 AM and I've opened seventeen browser tabs but haven't written anything.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: My mom called about visiting home, my friend needs relationship advice, my professor wants to meet about my grades, and I'm still in my pajamas from yesterday.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: The library is too loud but my room is too distracting and I don't know where to even start with this research paper so I'm just scrolling instead.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I took my medication this morning but my brain still feels like seventeen things are happening at once and I can't grab onto any of them.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: My laundry pile is becoming a permanent fixture, my dishes are multiplying, and I genuinely don't remember the last time I showered.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I have the list of things to do but looking at it makes me feel so paralyzed that I end up doing nothing at all.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: Someone asked me a simple question about dinner plans and I literally couldn't answer because I'm too busy thinking about three different assignments.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: The fluorescent lights in the study hall, the keyboard sounds, the people talking—it's all too much and my brain is just shutting down.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I have five notifications from my group project and I can't even open the messages because the thought of catching up makes me want to lie in bed forever.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: My doctor asked if the new medication dose is working and I couldn't even explain how I'm feeling because I don't have the words for this static in my head.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I need to eat, shower, study, email my professor, call my dad back, and start my laundry, and I'm just standing in my room doing nothing.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: There are so many small decisions I have to make today that I'm stuck on whether to have coffee or tea and I've been standing here for five minutes.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: My friends want to make plans, my family wants updates, my classes need my attention, and I'm simultaneously exhausted and unable to sleep.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I opened my laptop to do homework but ended up doom-scrolling for two hours because at least that doesn't require me to make any decisions.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: Everything feels urgent and important and impossible all at once, so my brain has decided we're just not doing anything today.
label: overwhelmed

sentence: I can feel the anxiety building about everything I'm not doing, which makes it even harder to do any of it.
label: overwhelmed
